LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION WITH PREJUDICE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS GEORGE AND BILLIE SCOTT ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE THEIR SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 18, 2018. DENYING THIS MOTION AND GRANTING THE SCOTTS' REQUEST TO FILE THEIR SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY FAVORING DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS AND WELLS FARGO HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO IT IF ITS MOTION IS DENIED AND THE SCOTTS' REQUEST IS GRANTED. THE SCOTTS' FAILURE TO FILE A TIMELY AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT WARRANT DEPRIVING THEM OF THE ABILITY TO PURSUE THEIR CLAIMS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiffs George Raymond Scott And Bille Andrea Scott For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Leigh O. Curran of The Law Firm of Anglin, Flewelling & Tryten LLP,
Tel. No. 1-626-535-1900 appearing for the Defendant by telephone appearance;
No other appearances on the matter.


RULING - The matter was called three times on the record waiting for Mr. Mellen to appear in court but was not able to do so. The court was informed by his assistant that he is submitting to the tentative ruling. The matter was argued. The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiffs George and Billie Scott's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. The Scotts are granted leave to file their second amended complaint no later than October 18, 2018. Granting this motion and denying defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to dismiss comports with California's strong policy favoring deciding cases on their merits and Wells Fargo has not identified any legal prejudice to it if its motion is denied and the Scotts' request is granted. The Scotts' failure to file a timely amended complaint does not warrant depriving them of the ability to pursue their claims. The amount of $1,250 will be awarded as a condition of granting leave to amend to be paid by Plaintiff or his counsel within 60 days. Failure to pay within that time period is not a ground to seek reconsideration of the granting leave to amend but it would be a credit for a favorable judgment for Wells Fargo Bank. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply wwith CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
